decisions.summary: Render PanOpenInPicker in two surfaces: CommandDeck OverviewTab (next to existing workspace.data.path line at OverviewTab.tsx:912) and the IssueDrawer workspace section (via WorkspaceStatusOverview). Reuses the existing workspace.path data (no new fetch) and matches the #966 PRD intent of 'split-button next to workspace path'.

Quickview design — ship Option A (Shift+hover popover with Shiki-highlighted snippet). Click already opens-in-editor; modifier disambiguates. Shift was chosen over Ctrl/Cmd (conventionally opens background) and Alt (often OS-claimed). Option B (pinned side panel) and Option C (modal) deferred to follow-up issues.

Hover trigger (not click trigger) for Quickview — Shift+hover. Avoids overloading the click handler that already means 'open in editor', so no disambiguation logic on click is needed. Popover dismisses on mouse-out.

Add a new pan.readWorkspaceFile RPC for Quickview. Server validates the resolved absolute path stays inside the workspace root (path-traversal guard) and rejects anything outside. Returns text plus an extension-derived lang hint. File-size truncation at 256 KiB.

Keybindings — MVP hardcodes `editor.openFavorite` as Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O exposed through a minimal stub config (single key in a const map). NOT a full keybindings system port — T3Code's #2533 keybindings settings editor is explicitly out of scope and noted as a follow-up.

Editor registry expansion adds Kiro plus the full JetBrains family (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, CLion, Rider, RubyMine, RustRover, DataGrip, DataSpell, Aqua, PhpStorm) verbatim from T3Code's `OpenInPicker.tsx` resolveOptions list. Command names + supportsGoto flags ported as-is.

T3Code port preserves file and identifier names with only t3→pan renames: new modules land at `src/dashboard/frontend/src/markdown-links.ts`, `src/dashboard/frontend/src/filePathDisplay.ts`, `src/dashboard/frontend/src/contextMenuFallback.ts`, and a new `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/MarkdownFileLink.tsx`. Keeps future upstream merges cheap (#703 drift tracker).

ChatMarkdown gains an optional `cwd?: string` prop. The `a()` renderer delegates to `MarkdownFileLink` when href looks like a file path AND `cwd` is provided; otherwise falls back to the existing safe-external-link renderer. Backwards-compatible — callsites without `cwd` keep current behavior until they are updated.

Context menu uses the web-fallback path only. T3Code's native bridge (`api.contextMenu.show`) does not exist in Panopticon (no desktop bridge yet), so `contextMenuFallback.ts` ships as the only implementation. The bridge call site is wired through a thin shim so a future native bridge can drop in without re-touching the chip component.
Auto-mode chosen Shiki for Quickview syntax highlighting (already a dependency — used in CodeBlock at ChatMarkdown.tsx:276 and SpecialistLogViewer/PlanDAG/etc.). No new dep needed.

hazards.summary: PAN-966 already shipped the server launcher (`open.ts`, `editor.ts`) and the `PanOpenInPicker` component, but the picker is rendered NOWHERE today. Naive wire-up will surface the component twice (Overview + Drawer) — make sure both surfaces consume the same `editorPreferences` localStorage key so a pick in one surface is reflected in the other.
hazards.mitigation: Both surfaces import `getPreferredEditor` / `setPreferredEditor` from `src/dashboard/frontend/src/editorPreferences.ts` (already does this) — no new state. Test that selection in Drawer is immediately reflected in OverviewTab on next render.

hazards.summary: Path-traversal in `pan.readWorkspaceFile`. A crafted relative path like `../../etc/passwd` could escape the workspace root if validation is naive.
hazards.mitigation: Resolve the requested path with `path.resolve(workspaceRoot, requested)` then assert the resolved path starts with `workspaceRoot + path.sep` (or equals it). Reject with PanRpcError code `PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE` otherwise. Symlinks: also `realpath` the result and re-check, to defeat symlink escapes. Cover both in tests.

hazards.summary: T3Code's `resolveMarkdownFileLinkMeta()` distinguishes three path forms (targetPath / displayPath / filePath). Getting the triple wrong means 'Copy relative path' copies an absolute path, or 'Open in editor' opens the wrong line. This is the foundation the chip menu encodes assumptions about — easy to get subtly wrong.
hazards.mitigation: Port verbatim from T3Code's `apps/web/src/markdown-links.ts` on `origin/main`. Add tests covering: absolute path with :line:col, absolute path bare, workspace-relative path, path with no workspace match (bail-out). Bead `port-markdown-links` is flagged requiresInspection: true so the inspector validates the triple semantics before downstream beads depend on them.

hazards.summary: ChatMarkdown is used in 8+ places in MessagesTimeline.tsx and in PlanCard, etc. Threading `cwd` to every callsite is a wide blast radius — easy to miss one, in which case that surface silently keeps the old naive `<a>` renderer.
hazards.mitigation: After adding the `cwd` prop, run `grep -n '<ChatMarkdown' src/dashboard/frontend/src` and audit every callsite. Where cwd is unavailable (e.g. system summaries), pass `undefined` explicitly so the typecheck distinguishes 'forgot to thread' from 'intentionally omitted'. Bead `chatmarkdown-integration` is flagged requiresInspection: true.

hazards.summary: Quickview popover positioning — anchoring next to the chip in a scrollable chat timeline must handle viewport edges (chip near right/bottom edge clips the popover). Naive `position: absolute` fails.
hazards.mitigation: Use the same positioning approach as the existing model/effort pickers (`src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/usePickerPosition.ts`). Reuse it rather than rolling new logic. Acceptance criterion explicitly mentions edge cases.

hazards.summary: Keybindings — adding a global `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O` could collide with browser shortcuts (Cmd+Shift+O in Chrome opens Bookmarks). Users will be annoyed.
hazards.mitigation: Scope the listener to the dashboard root via React event handler, not `window.addEventListener('keydown')`, and call `preventDefault()` when matched. Document the binding in the chosen keybinding's tooltip. If the collision proves problematic in UAT, change the default to Cmd/Ctrl+Alt+O before merge.

hazards.summary: Editor detection is cached at PanOpenLive layer construction (`open.ts:97` resolves editors once at boot). Adding Kiro/JetBrains to the registry doesn't take effect until the dashboard server restarts — agents may report missing editors after `pan up` until the user restarts.
hazards.mitigation: Document in the bead acceptance criteria that a dashboard restart is required to pick up the expanded registry. (No code change needed — the cache strategy is intentional, since `which` calls are slow on cold filesystems.)

hazards.summary: Browser-based UAT is needed for this feature (chips, context menu, hover popover, picker dropdown). The acceptance criteria can't be verified by typecheck/lint/unit tests alone.
hazards.mitigation: Plan acceptance criteria are written to be observable via Playwright. The test role MUST use an ISOLATED Playwright browser instance (per CLAUDE.md / planning rules) — no shared session, no dependence on another agent's browser state. Login/setup must be reproducible inside the isolated session.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1370: Editor & file-navigation integration: wire up Open picker, port MarkdownFileLink chip menu, add file Quickview, expand editor registry

Auto-mode planning completed. Confirmed PAN-966 already shipped server launcher + dead PanOpenInPicker component; ChatMarkdown.tsx:280 renders all links as naive external <a>. WorkspaceInfo.path is the cwd data source; OverviewTab.tsx:912 already renders it and has a primitive vscode:// link to replace. Decided to render picker in OverviewTab + IssueDrawer, ship Quickview Option A (Shift+hover Shiki popover), add new pan.readWorkspaceFile RPC with path-traversal guard, hardcode Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O for editor.openFavorite. 15 beads planned across 5 phases (registry, picker wire-up, port utilities, chip+menu integration, Quickview). 4 beads flagged requiresInspection (registry shape, path-triple semantics, ChatMarkdown integration, readFile RPC security).
